title: The Golden Statue Plot
author: Elisabetta Dami
desc: The pirate cats sail to Mouse Island to steal the golden Statue of Liberty, but clever mice and a flea army send them packing! 🏆

q.en: Who is the narrator of this story?
q.zh: 这本书的故事是谁来讲的？

explain.en: Geronimo introduces himself as the narrator who runs The Rodent's Gazette, the famous newspaper on Mouse Island.
explain.zh: 杰罗尼摩在书中自我介绍，他是老鼠岛著名报纸《鼠民公报》的社长，也是故事的叙述者。

q.en: Why did Geronimo wear sunglasses everywhere he went?
q.zh: 杰罗尼摩为什么走到哪儿都戴着墨镜？

options.en: He fell asleep in the sun with them on, leaving white marks around his eyes
options.zh: 他戴着墨镜在太阳下睡着了，眼睛周围留下了一圈白印

options.en: He was a famous movie star on vacation
options.zh: 他是个正在度假的著名电影明星

options.en: He had lost his regular glasses
options.zh: 他弄丢了自己的普通眼镜

options.en: He was going to a costume party

explain.en: He fell asleep on the beach with his sunglasses on, so he woke up with silly white marks around his eyes, like a reverse panda.
explain.zh: 他在海滩上戴着墨镜睡着了，醒来时眼睛周围留下了一圈可笑的白印，活像一只反过来的熊猫。

q.en: What caused the total blackout in New Mouse City?
q.zh: 新老鼠城为什么会彻底停电？

options.en: Mice kept all the lights on because of the sunglasses trend
options.zh: 因为墨镜风潮，大家整天整夜都开着灯

options.en: A big storm hit the island
The pirate cats attacked the city

explain.en: Geronimo's sunglasses trend spread, and mice kept the lights blazing day and night, using so much electricity that the city had a total blackout.
explain.zh: 杰罗尼摩的墨镜风潮传开了，老鼠们白天黑夜都开着灯，耗电太多，整座城市彻底停电了。

q.en: Who is the king of the pirate cats?

options.zh: 卡塔托尼亚的卡塔尔多内三世

explain.en: Catardone III of Catatonia is described as the king of the pirate cats who lives at Fort Feline on Cat Island.
explain.zh: 卡塔托尼亚的卡塔尔多内三世被描写为海盗猫的国王，住在猫岛上的猫咪堡垒。

q.en: What did Dr. Redfur claim the Mousey Liberty statue was made of?
q.zh: 红毛博士说自由鼠小姐雕像是用什么做的？

explain.en: Dr. Redfur insisted the statue was made of gold simply because it was yellow, which is her 'scientific proof'.
explain.zh: 红毛博士坚称雕像是黄金做的，理由仅仅是它是黄色的，这就是她所谓的“科学证据”。

q.en: Who is Geronimo's secret feline fan that loves reading his books?
q.zh: 谁是偷偷爱读杰罗尼摩书、崇拜他的猫粉丝？

explain.en: Samantha Wildwhiskers, Oscar's eldest daughter, secretly reads Geronimo's books and sends him messages by carrier pigeon.
explain.zh: 萨曼莎是奥斯卡的大女儿，她偷偷读杰罗尼摩的书，还用信鸽给他送信。

q.en: What clever idea did Benjamin have to stop the pirate cats?
q.zh: 本杰明想出了什么妙计来阻止海盗猫？

options.en: Attack them with an army of fleas
options.zh: 用一支跳蚤大军去攻击它们

explain.en: Benjamin realized that cats fear fleas the most, so they used a flea army to drive the cats away from the statue.
explain.zh: 本杰明想到猫最怕跳蚤，于是他们用一支跳蚤大军把猫从雕像边赶跑了。

q.en: Where was Geronimo standing when he said, 'Sorry to disturb you ...'?
q.zh: 杰罗尼摩说“抱歉打扰一下……”时，他站在哪里？

options.en: On a balcony behind the crown of Mousey Liberty
options.zh: 在自由鼠小姐王冠后面的一个小阳台上

options.en: On the deck of The Black Hurricane
options.zh: 在“黑色飓风号”的甲板上

options.en: In his office at The Rodent's Gazette
options.zh: 在《鼠民公报》的办公室里

explain.en: Geronimo spoke from a small balcony behind the crown on top of the Mousey Liberty statue.
explain.zh: 杰罗尼摩站在自由鼠小姐雕像顶端、王冠后面的一个小阳台上说话。

q.en: Who helped throw the cannonballs into the sea so the cats could not fire them?
q.zh: 是谁把炮弹丢进海里，让猫没法开炮的？

options.zh: 小猫咪、小侦察和奥斯卡
特尔西拉、瑟格西和基勒

explain.en: Oscar helped the twins Kitty and Scout throw the cannonballs into the sea to stop the cats from knocking down the statue.
explain.zh: 奥斯卡帮双胞胎小猫咪和小侦察把炮弹丢进海里，阻止猫炸倒雕像。

q.en: Why was the statue NOT actually made of gold?
q.zh: 为什么雕像其实根本不是黄金做的？

options.en: It was just yellow, like cheese
options.zh: 它只是黄色的，像奶酪一样

explain.en: Geronimo yelled that the statue was just yellow like cheese, the mice's favorite food, not real gold.
explain.zh: 杰罗尼摩大喊，雕像只是黄色的，像老鼠最爱吃的奶酪，并不是真的黄金。
